annotations.label: Separated by neighbourhoods

anchor.snippet: can be separated by neighbourhoods if there exists a neighbourhood

annotations.note: `SeparatedNhds` defines two sets being contained in disjoint open neighbourhoods, generalizing the two-point version (`Disjoint (𝓝 x) (𝓝 y)`).

annotations.label: Hausdorff space

anchor.snippet: is a Hausdorff space if any two distinct points in

annotations.note: `T2Space` requires that any two distinct points have disjoint open neighbourhoods, exactly the Hausdorff definition.

annotations.label: Preregular space

anchor.snippet: is a preregular space if any two topologically distinguishable points

annotations.note: `R1Space` is defined so that topologically distinguishable (non-specializing) points have disjoint neighbourhoods, i.e. preregularity.

annotations.label: Hausdorff iff preregular and Kolmogorov

anchor.snippet: A topological space is Hausdorff if and only if it is both preregular

annotations.note: `R1Space.t2Space_iff_t0Space` gives that a preregular space is T₂ iff it is T₀ (Kolmogorov), and an instance `[R1Space][T0Space] : T2Space` supplies the converse.

annotations.label: Preregular iff Kolmogorov quotient is Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: A topological space is preregular if and only if its Kolmogorov quotient is Hausdorff

annotations.note: `SeparationQuotient.t2Space_iff : T2Space (SeparationQuotient X) ↔ R1Space X`, where `SeparationQuotient` is the Kolmogorov quotient.

annotations.label: Characterizations of Hausdorff spaces

anchor.snippet: the following are equivalent

annotations.note: The individual equivalences (closed diagonal, disjoint nhds `t2Space_iff_disjoint_nhds`, unique net/filter limits `t2_iff_nhds`/`t2_iff_ultrafilter`) are all formalized though not bundled as one TFAE.

annotations.label: Real numbers are Hausdorff

anchor.section: Examples of Hausdorff and non-Hausdorff spaces
anchor.snippet: the real numbers (under the standard metric topology on real numbers) are a Hausdorff space

annotations.note: ℝ carries a `MetricSpace`/`MetrizableSpace` structure, so `t2Space_of_metrizableSpace` yields a `T2Space ℝ` instance.

annotations.label: Metric spaces are Hausdorff

anchor.section: Examples of Hausdorff and non-Hausdorff spaces
anchor.snippet: all metric spaces are Hausdorff

annotations.note: Every `MetrizableSpace` (hence every `MetricSpace`) is a `T2Space` via `t2Space_of_metrizableSpace`.

annotations.label: T1 but not Hausdorff: cofinite/cocountable

anchor.section: Examples of Hausdorff and non-Hausdorff spaces
anchor.snippet: A simple example of a topology that is

annotations.note: `CofiniteTopology X` has a `T1Space` instance and an `IrreducibleSpace` instance for infinite `X` (which precludes T₂), matching this example.

annotations.label: Pseudometric spaces are preregular not Hausdorff

anchor.section: Examples of Hausdorff and non-Hausdorff spaces
anchor.snippet: Pseudometric spaces typically are not Hausdorff

annotations.note: Pseudometrizable spaces are `RegularSpace`, hence `R1Space` (preregular) by the `RegularSpace → R1Space` instance; the non-T₀ (non-Hausdorff) aspect is not a separate lemma.

annotations.label: Non-preregular spaces in algebra/geometry

anchor.section: Examples of Hausdorff and non-Hausdorff spaces
anchor.snippet: non-preregular spaces are encountered much more frequently

annotations.note: This is an informal remark about Zariski/scheme topologies, not a formal statement.

annotations.label: US spaces

anchor.section: Examples of Hausdorff and non-Hausdorff spaces
anchor.snippet: Such spaces are called US spaces

annotations.note: No notion of US space (unique sequential limits) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: US equals weakly Hausdorff for sequential spaces

anchor.section: Examples of Hausdorff and non-Hausdorff spaces
anchor.snippet: this notion is equivalent to being weakly Hausdorff

annotations.note: Neither US spaces nor weakly Hausdorff spaces are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Subspaces and products of Hausdorff are Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: Subspaces and products of Hausdorff spaces are Hausdorff

annotations.note: `Prod.t2Space`, `Pi.t2Space`, and the `T2Space (Subtype p)` instance cover products and subspaces of Hausdorff spaces.

annotations.label: Every space is a quotient of a Hausdorff space

anchor.snippet: every topological space can be realized as the quotient of some Hausdorff space

annotations.note: No such universal construction realizing an arbitrary space as a quotient of a Hausdorff space was found.

annotations.label: Hausdorff spaces are T1

anchor.snippet: meaning that each singleton is a closed set

annotations.note: `T2Space.t1Space` is the instance that every Hausdorff space is T₁.

annotations.label: Preregular spaces are R0

anchor.snippet: Similarly, preregular spaces are R

annotations.note: An anonymous instance `[R1Space X] : R0Space X` (Basic.lean:889) shows preregular spaces are R₀.

annotations.label: Every Hausdorff space is sober

anchor.snippet: Every Hausdorff space is a Sober space

annotations.note: `R1Space.quasiSober` makes every preregular (hence Hausdorff) space `QuasiSober`, and Hausdorff is also T₀, giving soberness (Mathlib states sober as `QuasiSober` + `T0Space`).

annotations.label: Compact sets are closed in Hausdorff spaces

anchor.snippet: Another property of Hausdorff spaces is that each compact set is a closed set

annotations.note: `IsCompact.isClosed` states every compact set in a `T2Space` is closed.

annotations.label: Disjoint compact sets separated by neighbourhoods

anchor.snippet: every pair of disjoint compact sets can also be separated by neighborhoods

annotations.note: `SeparatedNhds.of_isCompact_isCompact` gives `SeparatedNhds` for two disjoint compact sets in a `T2Space`.

annotations.label: Locally compact preregular implies completely regular

anchor.snippet: any locally compact preregular space is completely regular

annotations.note: Mathlib has `[WeaklyLocallyCompactSpace][R1Space] → RegularSpace` (Regular.lean:132) but not the stronger conclusion of complete regularity.

annotations.label: Compact preregular spaces are normal

anchor.snippet: Compact preregular spaces are normal

annotations.note: `NormalSpace.of_compactSpace_r1Space` is the instance `[CompactSpace][R1Space] → NormalSpace`.

annotations.label: Locally compact Hausdorff is Tychonoff

anchor.snippet: every locally compact Hausdorff space is Tychonoff

annotations.note: Locally compact Hausdorff is shown regular (T₃) via Regular.lean:132 + `T2Space.r1Space`, but the Tychonoff/`T35Space` (completely regular) strengthening is not derived for this case.

annotations.label: Compact Hausdorff is normal Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: every compact Hausdorff space is normal Hausdorff

annotations.note: A compact `T2Space` is `R1Space`, so `NormalSpace.of_compactSpace_r1Space` gives normality (and T₁ persists, yielding `T4Space`).

annotations.label: Graph of continuous map into Hausdorff is closed

anchor.snippet: Then the graph of

annotations.note: No dedicated closed-graph lemma for continuous maps into T₂ spaces exists; it follows from `isClosed_eq` applied to `f ∘ Prod.fst` and `Prod.snd`.

annotations.label: Kernel is closed when codomain Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: is continuous and

annotations.note: The kernel {(x,x') | f x = f x'} being closed follows from `isClosed_eq` (and relates to `IsSeparatedMap.isClosed_eqLocus`), but is not stated as a standalone lemma.

annotations.label: Open surjection with closed kernel implies Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: is an open surjection and

annotations.note: The `←` direction of `t2Space_iff_of_isOpenQuotientMap` gives that an open quotient (continuous open surjection) with closed kernel `{q | π q.1 = π q.2}` has Hausdorff codomain.

annotations.label: Open quotient: Hausdorff iff kernel closed

anchor.snippet: is a continuous, open surjection (i.e. an open quotient map)

annotations.note: `t2Space_iff_of_isOpenQuotientMap : T2Space Y ↔ IsClosed {q : X×X | π q.1 = π q.2}` for an open quotient map `π`.

annotations.label: Equalizer into Hausdorff space is closed

anchor.snippet: then the equalizer

annotations.note: `isClosed_eq` states `{y | f y = g y}` is closed for continuous `f, g` into a `T2Space`.

annotations.label: Maps to Hausdorff determined on dense subsets

anchor.snippet: continuous functions into Hausdorff spaces are determined by their values on dense subsets

annotations.note: `Continuous.ext_on` shows two continuous maps into a `T2Space` agreeing on a dense set are equal (see also `Set.EqOn.closure`).

annotations.label: Closed surjection with compact fibers preserves Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: be a closed surjection such that

annotations.note: The proper-map infrastructure (closed + compact fibers) exists, but the conclusion that the Hausdorff property descends to the codomain is not formalized.

annotations.label: Quotient of compact Hausdorff equivalences

anchor.snippet: be a quotient map with

annotations.note: Related results exist (`IsQuotientMap.of_surjective_continuous` for compact-to-Hausdorff maps), but the TFAE for quotient maps out of a compact Hausdorff space is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Regular and Hausdorff spaces are preregular

anchor.snippet: All regular spaces are preregular, as are all Hausdorff spaces

annotations.note: `T2Space.r1Space` and the anonymous `RegularSpace → R1Space` instance (Regular.lean:165) cover both claims.

annotations.label: Locally compact Hausdorff is regular

anchor.snippet: a Hausdorff space that is also (say) locally compact will be regular

annotations.note: The instance `[WeaklyLocallyCompactSpace][R1Space] → RegularSpace` (Regular.lean:132) applies since locally compact Hausdorff is weakly locally compact and R₁.

annotations.label: Cauchy spaces are always preregular

anchor.snippet: are always preregular

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Cauchy-space notion, but uniform spaces are regular (`UniformSpace.to_regularSpace`) hence preregular.

annotations.label: Complete iff every Cauchy net has a limit

anchor.snippet: a space is complete if and only if every Cauchy net has at least one limit

annotations.note: `CompleteSpace` is defined as every Cauchy filter converging (equivalent to, and more general than, the Cauchy-net formulation; cf. `completeSpace_iff_ultrafilter`).

annotations.label: Hausdorff iff Cauchy nets have at most one limit

anchor.snippet: a space is Hausdorff if and only if every Cauchy net has at most one limit

annotations.note: `t2_iff_ultrafilter` (and `tendsto_nhds_unique`) characterize T₂ via uniqueness of filter/ultrafilter limits, the filter analogue of the Cauchy-net statement.

annotations.label: Continuous functions form a commutative C*-algebra

anchor.snippet: is a commutative C*-algebra

annotations.note: An instance `[CommCStarAlgebra A] : CommCStarAlgebra C(α, A)` (with `α` compact) makes `C(X, ℂ)` a commutative C*-algebra.

annotations.label: Banach–Stone theorem

anchor.snippet: one can recover the topology of the space

annotations.note: Banach–Stone appears only in bibliography references, not as a proven theorem in Mathlib.
